caption: Figure 1: Architecture of UtilityNet. The utility branch predicts the utility reward for each context- action pair, while the gating branch predicts whether UCB-bonus-based action selection should be activated.
description2: The architecture of UtilityNet diagrammatically depicts a utility branch predicting rewards for context-action pairs and a gating branch determining UCB-bonus-based action selection activation.
description3: Two-panel architecture diagram displays utility and gating branches with multiple linear layers and ReLU activations, connected by various embedding and MLP layers.
questions: What is the overall architecture of the UtilityNet system?
How does the system determine whether to use a bonus-based action selection?

caption: Figure 2: Reward comparison of NeuralUCB, RouteLLM-BERT, and two simple baselines, random and min-cost, under the simulated online routing setting.
description2: The figure compares the rewards of NeuralUCB, RouteLLM-BERT, and two simple baselines (random and min-cost) in a simulated online routing setting.
description3: Two-panel plot displays line charts comparing NeuralUCB, RouteLLM-BERT, random, and min-cost reward across slices, showing average and cumulative reward trends.
questions: How do the routing approaches differ in their average reward performance?
What is the general pattern of cumulative reward for each routing setting?

caption: Figure 3: Encoder ablation under the simulated online routing setting. We compare four text en- coders, multilingual-e5-large-instruct, all-mpnet-base-v2, Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B, and all-MiniLM-L6-v2, using both average reward and cumulative reward.
description2: Encoder ablation is plotted under a simulated online routing setting, comparing four text encoders: multilingual-e5-large-instruct, all-mpnet-base-v2, Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B, and all-MiniLM-L6-v2.
description3: Two-panel line chart compares four text encoders' average and cumulative reward across slices, with different colored lines representing each encoder.
questions: How do different embedding encoders affect average reward in this simulated setting?
What is the overall pattern of cumulative reward for the encoder types shown?

caption: Figure 4: Comparison between the proposed NeuralUCB policy and the max-quality reference in terms of cost and selected quality under the simulated online routing setting.
description2: The figure compares the proposed NeuralUCB policy with a max-quality reference in terms of cost and selected quality under a simulated online routing setting.
description3: Four-panel line charts compare NeuralUCB and max-quality reference data, displaying cost and quality trends across slices on the x-axis.
questions: What aspects of routing performance are being compared in this illustration?
Describe the general relationship between cost and quality during the routing process.?
